[108:53]Let me explain it very simply. Again, for people that still don't get it, because it's really not

[108:59]that complicated. I don't necessarily think we need to say that they're trying to kill the white race

[109:06]or maybe they are, but we don't even need to go there because it's really this simple.

[109:13]In the economy, there are people that work for a living. They use their time and they use their

[109:23]manual, manual meaning hands, labor. They use their skills. They learn a profession

[109:29]and they work to earn a living. There are other people that don't work to make a living.

[109:37]They earn their living through owning assets.

[109:42]They own productive assets, like real estate, businesses.

[109:46]They own productive capital.

[109:49]They own large amounts of capital.

[109:52]That's what capitalism is.

[109:53]It's when private hands own the productive capital.

[109:59]The businesses, the machinery, the real estate, the land.

[110:04]So there are those that work.

[110:06]There are those that go to work and provide their labor for money, and then there are

[110:11]those that earn money through owning productive assets.

[110:16]The largest expense for the capitalists, for those that own the capital, is the labor.

[110:25]If you own a business and make money from a business,

[110:29]you make profit from a business.

[110:32]The way that you make profit is by employing workers.

[110:38]And the workers work to bring in revenue,

[110:41]and that is how the capitalist makes a profit.

[110:45]Their largest cost in that equation is paying the laborers.

[110:51]Well, this is where immigration,

[110:54]becomes a variable. The more workers there are in the economy, the lower the wages are.

[111:01]The greater the supply, the lower the price, the lower the demand. So it is better for the

[111:09]capitalists to have as many workers competing in the country as possible to achieve the lowest wage

[111:16]and therefore the lowest costs and therefore the highest profit. So whether it is

[111:25]Agriculture, employing cheap, low-skilled labor from Latin America or whether it is tech, employing higher-skilled labor from India, they're paying for labor.

[111:40]And when they import labor from foreign countries with a lower standard of living, and when those workers depend upon their employer for residents in the United States,

[111:53]they can pay those workers a lower wage. And when they do it under the table,

[111:59]they could do it with no benefits, with, without the proper safety regulations,

[112:05]without providing healthcare, without having to provide for FICO, for Social Security and all

[112:12]that stuff. And so this is why across the board, the people that have the money

[112:21]the people that pay the politicians for their campaigns,

[112:26]this is why they always favor more immigration

[112:30]in a nutshell, in essence, to keep the labor cost down.

[112:35]And so what is being done to put it very simply,

[112:37]and there are other ways in which labor affects the economy

[112:40]or immigration affects the economy,

[112:42]the more people that are here, the more scarce housing becomes.

[112:46]If there's more people,

[112:48]Housing becomes more expensive because there is demand for housing, rising housing costs

[112:54]benefits those that own the homes, capital owners.

[113:04]The bigger all of the companies get, the bigger the stock market gets, that benefits those
